title: Size quorums so reads and writes intersect
description: Pick read and write quorums whose sizes guarantee overlap (R + W > N) so every read sees the latest acknowledged write.
body: Use when designing a quorum-replicated store or tuning Dynamo-style consistency knobs. Do not set R + W <= N when you need read-your-writes, and avoid even-sized voting sets that admit tie partitions. Actions: choose N, then require R + W > N and W > N/2 so write sets intersect each other and every read intersects the last write; record the chosen values and the consistency they buy. Validation signals include a proof that any read quorum shares at least one node with any write quorum, and a test that a read after an acked write never returns a stale value. Risk boundary: under-sized quorums return stale data that looks like a healthy success; require explicit sign-off before weakening R or W in production.
